PublisherSite.title: PublisherSite
PublisherSite.description: Represents a publisher's website where embedded audio players are tested.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PublisherSite entity.

name.description: The human-readable name of the publisher site (e.g., 'The Hill').

url.description: The base URL of the publisher's website (e.g., 'https://thehill.com/').

description.description: An optional description or notes about the publisher site.

contactEmail.description: Contact email for administrative purposes related to the publisher site.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the publisher site is currently active for QA testing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the publisher site record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the publisher site record was last updated.

Article.title: Article
Article.description: Represents a specific article page on a publisher site that contains an embedded audio player.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Article entity.

publisherSiteId.description: Reference to the PublisherSite this article belongs to. (Relationship: PublisherSite 1:N Article)

url.description: The full URL of the article page.

title.description: The title of the article, typically extracted from the page content.

externalBlogAudioId.description: Reference to the 'blogaudio_id' in the external PostgreSQL 'blogaudio' table, if a matching entry is found for this article. (Relationship: Article 1:1 ExternalBlogAudio)

lastQARunId.description: Reference to the most recent QA run performed specifically on this article. (Relationship: QARun 1:N Article)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the article record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the article record was last updated.

QARun.title: QARun
QARun.description: Represents a single execution of the QA automation process for a publisher site or specific article(s).

id.description: Unique identifier for the QARun entity.

publisherSiteId.description: Reference to the PublisherSite targeted by this QA run. (Relationship: PublisherSite 1:N QARun)

articleId.description: Reference to a specific Article targeted by this QA run. This field is null if the run targeted an entire site or multiple articles. (Relationship: Article 1:N QARun)

initiatedByUserId.description: Identifier of the user or system component (e.g., 'orchestrator') that triggered this QA run.

status.description: The current status of the QA run (e.g., 'pending', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled').

browserType.description: The browser type used for the QA run (e.g., 'chromium', 'firefox', 'webkit').

startTime.description: Timestamp when the QA run started.

endTime.description: Timestamp when the QA run ended. Null if still in progress.

overallPlayerHealthStatus.description: Overall summary status of player functionality tests for this run (e.g., 'pass', 'fail', 'warning', 'not_applicable').

overallAudioQualityStatus.description: Overall summary status of audio quality tests for this run (e.g., 'pass', 'fail', 'warning', 'not_applicable').

totalArticlesTested.description: The total number of articles included in this QA run.

playerTestsPassedCount.description: Count of player functionality test steps that passed.

playerTestsFailedCount.description: Count of player functionality test steps that failed.

audioTestsPassedCount.description: Count of audio quality test steps that passed.

audioTestsFailedCount.description: Count of audio quality test steps that failed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the QA run record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the QA run record was last updated.

PlayerTestResult.title: PlayerTestResult
PlayerTestResult.description: Stores the outcome and details of a single player functionality test step within a QA run.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerTestResult entity.

qaRunId.description: Reference to the QA run this test result belongs to. (Relationship: QARun 1:N PlayerTestResult)

articleId.description: Reference to the Article being tested. (Relationship: Article 1:N PlayerTestResult)

testStepName.description: The name of the specific player functionality test step (e.g., 'Player Loads', 'Audio Playback', 'Ad Validation').

status.description: The outcome status of the test step (e.g., 'pass', 'fail', 'skipped', 'in_progress').

browserType.description: The browser type used for this specific test step (e.g., 'chromium').

errorMessage.description: Detailed error message if the test step failed.

detectedPlayerState.description: The detected state of the audio player (e.g., 'Listen to this article', 'Audio coming soon', 'No player').

durationMilliseconds.description: The duration of this specific test step in milliseconds.

screenshotArtifactIds.description: References to screenshot artifacts captured during this player test step. (Relationship: PlayerTestResult 1:N Artifact)

consoleLogArtifactId.description: Reference to the console log artifact for this player test step. (Relationship: PlayerTestResult 1:1 Artifact)

networkLogArtifactId.description: Reference to the network log artifact for this player test step. (Relationship: PlayerTestResult 1:1 Artifact)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this player test result was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this player test result was last updated.

AudioTestResult.title: AudioTestResult
AudioTestResult.description: Stores the outcome and metrics of an audio quality test for an article's embedded audio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AudioTestResult entity.

qaRunId.description: Reference to the QA run this test result belongs to. (Relationship: QARun 1:N AudioTestResult)

articleId.description: Reference to the Article whose audio is being tested. (Relationship: Article 1:N AudioTestResult)

status.description: The outcome status of the audio quality test (e.g., 'pass', 'fail', 'skipped', 'in_progress').

errorMessage.description: Detailed error message if the audio quality test failed.

wordErrorRate.description: The Word Error Rate (WER) metric comparing transcribed audio to the canonical article text.

semanticSimilarityScore.description: A score representing the semantic similarity between the transcribed audio and the canonical article text.

speechConfidenceScore.description: An overall confidence score for the speech recognition and transcription process.

detectedIssues.description: A list of specific audio quality issues detected (e.g., 'mispronounced_word', 'missing_sentence', 'extra_words', 'silence_segment', 'clipped_audio').

audioTranscriptArtifactId.description: Reference to the artifact containing the speech-to-text transcription of the article's audio. (Relationship: AudioTestResult 1:1 Artifact)

finetunedArticleTextArtifactId.description: Reference to the artifact containing the 'article_body_finetuned' text used as the canonical source for comparison. (Relationship: AudioTestResult 1:1 Artifact)

rawAudioFileArtifactId.description: Reference to the artifact containing the raw audio file downloaded for transcription. (Relationship: AudioTestResult 1:1 Artifact)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this audio test result was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this audio test result was last updated.

BugReport.title: BugReport
BugReport.description: Represents a bug identified during a QA run, containing details necessary for reporting and resolution.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BugReport entity.

qaRunId.description: Reference to the QA run that identified this bug. (Relationship: QARun 1:N BugReport)

playerTestResultId.description: Reference to the specific PlayerTestResult that directly triggered this bug report, if applicable. (Relationship: PlayerTestResult 1:1 BugReport)

audioTestResultId.description: Reference to the specific AudioTestResult that directly triggered this bug report, if applicable. (Relationship: AudioTestResult 1:1 BugReport)

publisherSiteId.description: Reference to the PublisherSite where the bug was found. (Relationship: PublisherSite 1:N BugReport)

articleId.description: Reference to the Article where the bug was found. (Relationship: Article 1:N BugReport)

failedStep.description: A description of the specific test step or functionality that failed (e.g., 'Player did not load', 'Ad playback failed').

severity.description: The severity level of the bug (e.g., 'Critical', 'High', 'Medium', 'Low').

title.description: A concise summary or headline for the bug report.

description.description: A detailed description of the issue, including context, observed behavior, and expected behavior.

status.description: The current workflow status of the bug report (e.g., 'Open', 'Triaged', 'In Progress', 'Resolved', 'Closed').

assignedTo.description: The name of the QA engineer or team member assigned to this bug report (e.g., 'Shivani', 'Honey Grace').

browserType.description: The browser type in which the bug was observed (e.g., 'chromium').

reportedAt.description: Timestamp when the bug report was generated.

resolvedAt.description: Timestamp when the bug was marked as resolved. Null if not resolved.

screenshotArtifactIds.description: References to relevant screenshot artifacts that illustrate the bug. (Relationship: BugReport 1:N Artifact)

consoleLogArtifactId.description: Reference to the relevant console log artifact for debugging this bug. (Relationship: BugReport 1:1 Artifact)

networkLogArtifactId.description: Reference to the relevant network log artifact for debugging this bug. (Relationship: BugReport 1:1 Artifact)

audioTranscriptArtifactId.description: Reference to the relevant audio transcript artifact for audio-related bugs. (Relationship: BugReport 1:1 Artifact)

sessionReplayVideoArtifactId.description: Reference to a session replay video artifact, if available, for reproducing the bug. (Relationship: BugReport 1:1 Artifact)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the bug report record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the bug report record was last updated.

Artifact.title: Artifact
Artifact.description: Stores metadata about test-related files (e.g., screenshots, logs, transcripts) uploaded to object storage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Artifact entity.

qaRunId.description: Reference to the QA run during which this artifact was generated. (Relationship: QARun 1:N Artifact)

articleId.description: Reference to the Article associated with this artifact. (Relationship: Article 1:N Artifact)

description.description: A brief description of the artifact, providing context (e.g., 'Screenshot: Player before play button click').

s3Bucket.description: The name of the AWS S3 bucket where the artifact file is stored.

s3Key.description: The unique key (path) within the S3 bucket used to retrieve the artifact file.

capturedAt.description: Timestamp when the artifact was captured or generated.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the artifact record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the artifact record was last updated.

AgentActivity.title: AgentActivity
AgentActivity.description: Records the real-time status and activity of agents for monitoring purposes on the dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AgentActivity entity, potentially representing a specific agent instance or type.

agentName.description: The name of the agent (e.g., 'QA Orchestrator', 'Shivani Master Agent', 'Player Detection Agent').

status.description: The current operational status of the agent (e.g., 'online', 'idle', 'busy', 'error', 'offline').

lastHeartbeat.description: Timestamp of the last successful communication (heartbeat) received from the agent.

currentTask.description: An optional description of the task the agent is currently performing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the agent activity record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the agent activity record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores metadata about each publisher website. Access requires a QA engineer role. No direct parent authorization dependencies.
Stores details about articles on a specific publisher site. Includes denormalized 'publisherSiteId' for authorization independence.

params.name: publisherSiteId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent PublisherSite.

definition.description: Stores records of QA automation runs for a publisher site. Includes denormalized 'publisherSiteId' and 'initiatedByUserId' for authorization independence.

params.name: publisherSiteId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent PublisherSite.

params.name: qaRunId
params.description: Unique identifier for the QA run.

definition.description: Stores results for individual player functionality test steps within a QA run. **Requires denormalized 'publisherSiteId' field** for authorization independence. 'qaRunId' and 'articleId' are explicitly included in the schema.

params.name: publisherSiteId
params.description: Unique identifier of the PublisherSite where the QA run took place.

params.name: qaRunId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent QA Run.

definition.description: Stores results for individual audio quality test steps within a QA run. **Requires denormalized 'publisherSiteId' field** for authorization independence. 'qaRunId' and 'articleId' are explicitly included in the schema.

params.name: publisherSiteId
params.description: Unique identifier of the PublisherSite where the QA run took place.

params.name: qaRunId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent QA Run.

definition.description: Stores detailed bug reports generated during a QA run. Includes denormalized 'publisherSiteId', 'qaRunId', and 'articleId' for authorization independence.

params.name: publisherSiteId
params.description: Unique identifier of the PublisherSite where the bug was found.

params.name: qaRunId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent QA Run that identified the bug.

definition.description: Stores metadata about test-related files (e.g., screenshots, logs) uploaded to object storage. **Requires denormalized 'publisherSiteId' field** for authorization independence. 'qaRunId' and 'articleId' are explicitly included in the schema.

params.name: publisherSiteId
params.description: Unique identifier of the PublisherSite associated with the artifact.

params.name: qaRunId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent QA Run during which the artifact was generated.

definition.description: Records real-time status and activity of QA automation agents for monitoring. Access is global for authorized QA users. No hierarchical authorization.

params.name: agentId
params.description: Unique identifier for the AgentActivity record (e.g., agent instance ID).

definition.description: Stores UIDs of users who are authorized QA engineers. Used for database-backed access control (DBAC). Documents are typically empty, only their existence matters.

params.name: userId
params.description: The UID of the user who is a QA engineer.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure emphasizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy involves a hierarchical path structure combined with strategic denormalization of authorization context.

**Authorization Independence:**
*   For `PublisherSite`, it's a top-level collection, with access primarily controlled by global QA engineer roles (e.g., membership in `/roles_qa_engineers/{uid}`).
*   `Article` documents are nested under `/publisher_sites/{publisherSiteId}/articles/{articleId}`. The `Article` schema explicitly includes `publisherSiteId`, allowing rules to verify that `request.path.publisherSiteId == resource.data.publisherSiteId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `PublisherSite` document.
*   `QARun` documents are nested under `/publisher_sites/{publisherSiteId}/qa_runs/{qaRunId}`. The `QARun` schema explicitly includes `publisherSiteId` and `initiatedByUserId`, allowing rules to check path parameters against document data or user ID directly.
*   `PlayerTestResult`, `AudioTestResult`, `BugReport`, and `Artifact` documents are deeply nested under `/publisher_sites/{publisherSiteId}/qa_runs/{qaRunId}/<collectionName>/{docId}`. To ensure complete authorization independence, these documents **MUST** denormalize the `publisherSiteId` field into their respective documents, even if not explicitly present in the provided schema. `BugReport` already includes `publisherSiteId` and `qaRunId`, which is ideal. For `PlayerTestResult`, `AudioTestResult`, and `Artifact`, the `publisherSiteId` should be added to their schema to enable atomic rule evaluation and avoid `get()` calls.
*   The `qaRunId` is available via the path parameter and explicitly stored within the `PlayerTestResult`, `AudioTestResult`, `BugReport`, and `Artifact` schemas, allowing direct comparison in rules. This design eliminates all `get()` calls in security rules for authorization, making rules atomic and easier to debug.

**Querying with Authorization Predicates (QAPs):**
*   The structural segregation (Strategy B) ensures that `list` operations are inherently secure. For example, to retrieve all articles for a specific publisher site, a client must query `db.collection('publisher_sites/{publisherSiteId}/articles')`. The security rules can then verify that `request.path.publisherSiteId` matches the `publisherSiteId` wildcard in the path *and* the denormalized `publisherSiteId` field within the `Article` document (if explicitly denormalized for strict rule compliance). This prevents clients from performing broad, unauthorized queries that the rules would then attempt to filter.
*   Access to data like `PlayerTestResult` or `BugReport` for a specific QA run will require clients to specify the full hierarchical path, for instance, `/publisher_sites/{publisherSiteId}/qa_runs/{qaRunId}/bug_reports`. The rules can then ensure that the `request.auth.uid` has the necessary `qa_engineer` role and that the path parameters match the denormalized `publisherSiteId` and `qaRunId` fields in the `resource.data`.
*   Global access for QA engineers is managed through a dedicated `/roles_qa_engineers/{uid}` collection. Any authenticated `request.auth.uid` that has a corresponding document in this collection can be granted read/write access to relevant data, allowing the QA Dashboard to fetch all necessary data for its displays without complex filtering at the rule level.
